# Delhivery SDK — Full Reference

> TypeScript SDK for the Delhivery B2C logistics API.
> Package: `delhivery-sdk`
> Repo: https://github.com/delhivery-sdk/delhivery-sdk
> Docs: https://delhivery-sdk.pages.dev

---

## Installation

```bash
npm install delhivery-sdk
# yarn add delhivery-sdk
# pnpm add delhivery-sdk
```

Requires Node.js >= 18 (uses native `fetch`). Works in any environment with the Fetch API (Node 18+, Bun, Deno, Cloudflare Workers, browsers — though exposing tokens client-side is not recommended).

---

## Client Construction

```typescript
import { DelhiveryClient, STAGING_BASE_URL } from "delhivery-sdk";

// Production
const client = new DelhiveryClient({ token: "YOUR_API_TOKEN" });

// Staging / sandbox
const staging = new DelhiveryClient({
  token: "YOUR_STAGING_TOKEN",
  baseUrl: STAGING_BASE_URL, // "https://staging-express.delhivery.com"
});

// Custom timeout (default: 30 000 ms)
const client2 = new DelhiveryClient({ token: "...", timeout: 60_000 });
```

Authentication header sent on every request: `Authorization: Token <token>`

---

## Serviceability — `client.serviceability`

### check(params) → Promise<ServiceabilityResult[]>

Check if a destination pincode is serviceable from an origin pincode.

Params:
- `origin_pin: string` — pickup pincode
- `destination_pin: string` — delivery pincode
- `payment_mode: "Prepaid" | "COD"` — payment mode
- `weight: number` — weight in kg

Result fields per item:
- `cod: boolean` — COD available
- `delivery: boolean` — delivery serviceable
- `pickup: boolean` — pickup available
- `destination_sensitive: boolean` — restricted zone
- `origin_city: string`
- `destination_city: string`

```typescript
const [s] = await client.serviceability.check({
  origin_pin: "400001",
  destination_pin: "560001",
  payment_mode: "COD",
  weight: 1.0,
});
if (!s?.cod) throw new Error("COD not available");
```

### expectedTAT(params) → Promise<TATResponse>

Get expected delivery date (TAT) between two pincodes.

Params: `origin_pin`, `destination_pin`, optional `payment_mode`

Result: `{ estimated_date: string, origin: string, destination: string, tat?: number }`

```typescript
const tat = await client.serviceability.expectedTAT({
  origin_pin: "400001",
  destination_pin: "110001",
});
console.log(tat.estimated_date); // "2024-06-05"
```

---

## Orders — `client.orders`

### create(shipments) → Promise<CreateOrderResponse>

Create one or more shipments (Prepaid, COD, or Pickup).

Key shipment fields:
- `client_order_id: string` — your unique order ID (required)
- `order: string` — order date YYYY-MM-DD (required)
- `pickup_location: string` — warehouse name, must exist in your account (required)
- `payment_mode: "Prepaid" | "COD" | "Pickup"` (required)
- `total_amount: number` — invoice value (required)
- `weight: number` — kg (required)
- `name`, `phone`, `add`, `city`, `state`, `pin`, `country` — delivery address (all required)
- `cod_amount: number` — amount to collect; required when payment_mode = "COD"
- `waybill?: string` — leave empty to auto-assign
- `length?, breadth?, height?: number` — dimensions in cm
- `invoice?: string`, `e_way_bill_no?: string`, `gst_tin?: string`
- `shipment_items?: ShipmentItem[]` — line-item breakdown

Response: `{ packages: [{ waybill, client_order_id, status: "Success"|"Error", remarks }] }`

```typescript
const res = await client.orders.create([{
  client_order_id: "ORD-001",
  order: "2024-06-01",
  pickup_location: "Mumbai Warehouse",
  payment_mode: "Prepaid",
  total_amount: 999,
  weight: 0.5,
  name: "Rahul Sharma",
  phone: "9876543210",
  add: "123, MG Road",
  city: "Bengaluru",
  state: "Karnataka",
  pin: "560001",
  country: "India",
}]);
const waybill = res.packages[0]?.waybill;
```

### update(shipments) → Promise<CreateOrderResponse>

Update fields on existing shipments. Pass only fields to change.

Required: `waybill: string`. All other fields optional (`name`, `phone`, `add`, `city`, `state`, `pin`, `weight`, `payment_mode`, `cod_amount`, etc.)

### cancel(waybills) → Promise<CancelOrderResponse[]>

Cancel shipments by waybill. Only works before pickup.

```typescript
await client.orders.cancel(["WB001", "WB002"]);
```

### updateEWaybill(params) → Promise<EWaybillUpdateResponse>

Attach/update GST e-waybill number.

```typescript
await client.orders.updateEWaybill({
  waybill: "WB001",
  e_way_bill_no: "EWB1234567890",
});
```

---

## Tracking — `client.tracking`

### trackOne(waybill) → Promise<TrackingResponse>

```typescript
const data = await client.tracking.trackOne("WB001");
const s = data.ShipmentData[0]?.Shipment;
console.log(s?.Status, s?.["Delivery Date"]);
```

### trackMany(waybills) → Promise<TrackingResponse>

Up to 10 waybills per call.

```typescript
const data = await client.tracking.trackMany(["WB001", "WB002"]);
```

### track(params) → Promise<TrackingResponse>

Low-level. Accepts `waybill` (comma-separated) or `ref_ids` (your client order IDs, comma-separated).

Shipment response fields include:
`Status`, `Status DateTime`, `Origin City`, `Destination City`, `Pickup Date`, `Delivery Date`, `Expected Delivery Date`, `Customer Name`, `Customer Pin`, `Waybill`, `ReferenceNo`, `COD Amount`, `Scans[]`

Each scan: `{ "SL Date", City, Location, "Scan Type", Scan, Instructions }`

---

## Shipping — `client.shipping`

### calculateCost(params) → Promise<ShippingCostResponse>

Estimate shipping charge between pincodes.

Params: `origin_pin`, `destination_pin`, `payment_mode`, `weight`, optional `length/breadth/height`, `cod_amount`

Result fields: `total_amount`, `freight_charge`, `cod_charge`, `fuel_surcharge`, `tax`, etc.

### fetchWaybills(count) → Promise<WaybillFetchResponse>

Pre-fetch waybill numbers (1–100 at a time).

```typescript
const { waybill_list } = await client.shipping.fetchWaybills(10);
```

### generateLabel(params) → Promise<ArrayBuffer>

Generate shipping label PDF. `params.waybill` can be comma-separated for bulk labels.

```typescript
const pdf = await client.shipping.generateLabel({ waybill: "WB001" });
import { writeFileSync } from "fs";
writeFileSync("label.pdf", Buffer.from(pdf));
```

### schedulePickup(params) → Promise<PickupScheduleResponse>

Schedule a courier pickup.

Params: `pickup_date` (YYYY-MM-DD), `expected_package_quantity`, optional `client_warehouse_code`, `waybill_list`

---

## Warehouses — `client.warehouses`

The `pickup_location` in order creation must match an existing warehouse `name`.

### create(params) → Promise<WarehouseResponse>

Required: `name`, `address`, `city`, `state`, `country`, `pin`, `phone`
Optional: `address2`, `email`, `return_address`, `return_city`, `return_pin`, `gst_no`

### update(params) → Promise<WarehouseResponse>

Required: `name`. All other fields optional.

---

## NDR — `client.ndr`

### update(params) → Promise<NDRUpdateResponse>

Handle a Non-Delivery Report.

Params:
- `waybill: string` (required)
- `action: "re-attempt" | "return" | "confirm" | "address-update" | "phone-update" | "reschedule"` (required)
- `reattempt_date?: string` — YYYY-MM-DD, for re-attempt/reschedule
- `updated_address?: string` — for address-update
- `updated_contact?: string` — for phone-update
- `seller_note?: string`

### rvpQC(params) → Promise<RVPQCResponse>

QC for reverse pickup. Params: `waybill`, `qc_status: "Pass"|"Fail"`, `otp?`, `remarks?`

---

## Documents — `client.documents`

### download(params) → Promise<ArrayBuffer>

Download a document PDF.

Params:
- `waybill: string`
- `type: "label" | "invoice" | "manifest" | "pod"`

```typescript
const pdf = await client.documents.download({ waybill: "WB001", type: "pod" });
```

---

## Webhooks

Register your endpoint in the Delhivery One portal. Delhivery POSTs JSON on every scan event.

Payload type: `WebhookEvent` (exported from the package)

Fields: `waybill`, `status`, `"Status DateTime"`, `City`, `"Scan Type"`, `Scan`, `ReferenceNo?`, `Instructions?`

Common status values: `Manifested`, `In Transit`, `Out for Delivery`, `Delivered`, `NDR`, `RTO Initiated`, `RTO Delivered`, `Lost`

```typescript
import type { WebhookEvent } from "delhivery-sdk";

app.post("/webhooks/delhivery", (req, res) => {
  res.sendStatus(200); // always respond quickly
  const events: WebhookEvent[] = Array.isArray(req.body) ? req.body : [req.body];
  for (const e of events) {
    if (e.status === "Delivered") markDelivered(e.waybill);
  }
});
```

---

## Error Handling

All errors are exported from `delhivery-sdk`:

| Class | Condition |
|---|---|
| `DelhiveryError` | Generic API error — has `.statusCode: number` and `.raw: unknown` |
| `DelhiveryAuthError` | 401/403 — bad or expired token |
| `DelhiveryRateLimitError` | 429 — slow down |
| `DelhiveryNetworkError` | Network/timeout failure — has `.cause` |

```typescript
import { DelhiveryAuthError, DelhiveryNetworkError, DelhiveryError } from "delhivery-sdk";

try {
  await client.tracking.trackOne("WB001");
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof DelhiveryAuthError) { /* bad token */ }
  else if (err instanceof DelhiveryNetworkError) { /* network down */ }
  else if (err instanceof DelhiveryError) {
    console.log(err.statusCode, err.raw);
  }
}
```

---

## Full type exports

All TypeScript types are exported from the package root:

`DelhiveryClientOptions`, `PaymentMode`, `ProductType`, `Address`,
`PincodeServiceabilityParams`, `ServiceabilityResult`, `TATParams`, `TATResponse`,
`Shipment`, `ShipmentItem`, `CreateOrderResponse`, `CreateOrderShipmentResult`,
`UpdateShipment`, `CancelOrderResponse`, `EWaybillUpdateParams`, `EWaybillUpdateResponse`,
`TrackingParams`, `TrackingResponse`, `TrackingScan`, `TrackingShipment`,
`CreateWarehouseParams`, `UpdateWarehouseParams`, `WarehouseResponse`,
`ShippingCostParams`, `ShippingCostResponse`, `WaybillFetchResponse`,
`LabelGenerateParams`, `PickupScheduleParams`, `PickupScheduleResponse`,
`NDRAction`, `NDRUpdateParams`, `NDRUpdateResponse`,
`RVPQCParams`, `RVPQCResponse`, `WebhookEvent`
